subject: ap-chemistry
unit_name: Molecular and Ionic Compound Structure and Properties

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: A bond formed by the sharing of electron pairs between two nonmetal atoms

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: A bond formed by electrostatic attraction between oppositely charged ions (metal + nonmetal)

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: A bond in which both electrons come from the same atom (coordinate covalent)

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: A model for predicting molecular geometry based on minimizing repulsion between electron domains

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Linear geometry
cards.answer: Molecular shape with 2 bonding domains and 0 lone pairs; bond angle = $180°$

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Trigonal planar geometry
cards.answer: Molecular shape with 3 bonding domains and 0 lone pairs; bond angle = $120°$

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Tetrahedral geometry
cards.answer: Molecular shape with 4 bonding domains and 0 lone pairs; bond angle = $109.5°$

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Bent (angular) geometry
cards.answer: Molecular shape resulting from 2 bonding + 1 or 2 lone pairs; bond angle $< 120°$ or $< 109.5°$

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Trigonal pyramidal geometry
cards.answer: Molecular shape with 3 bonding domains and 1 lone pair; bond angle $\approx 107°$; example: $\text{NH}_3$

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: A picture of a molecule showing atoms, bonding pairs, and lone pairs, satisfying the octet rule

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The equal distribution of electron density in two or more resonance structures, creating identical partial bonds

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: A measure of the hypothetical charge on an atom in a molecule if all bonds were fully ionic

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: $\text{FC} = V - L - \frac{B}{2}$ where $V$ = valence electrons, $L$ = lone pair electrons, $B$ = bonding electrons

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The mixing of atomic orbitals to form new hybrid orbitals for bonding

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: $sp^3$ hybridization
cards.answer: One $s$ + three $p$ orbitals combine to form four equivalent $sp^3$ orbitals; tetrahedral geometry

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: $sp^2$ hybridization
cards.answer: One $s$ + two $p$ orbitals combine to form three equivalent $sp^2$ orbitals; trigonal planar; one unhybridized $p$ orbital available for $\pi$ bonding

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: $sp$ hybridization
cards.answer: One $s$ + one $p$ combine to form two equivalent $sp$ orbitals; linear geometry; two unhybridized $p$ orbitals available for $\pi$ bonding

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: A bond formed by head-on overlap of atomic orbitals along the internuclear axis

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: A bond formed by side-to-side overlap of $p$ orbitals, with electron density above and below the bond axis

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The energy required to separate an ionic compound into its gaseous ions

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: A measure of bond strength; the energy required to break one mole of a bond in the gas phase

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The distance between the nuclei of two bonded atoms at their lowest potential energy

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: As bond order increases (single → double → triple), bond length _____ and bond energy _____

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: A molecule with an uneven distribution of charge due to different electronegativities of bonded atoms

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: A molecule that has polar bonds but zero net dipole moment due to symmetry

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The ratio of moles of compound to kilograms of solvent

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Octet rule
cards.answer: Atoms tend to form bonds to achieve 8 electrons in their valence shell (like noble gases)

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: A species with more than 8 electrons around the central atom (e.g., $\text{PCl}_5$, $\text{SF}_6$)

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: A species with fewer than 8 electrons around the central atom (e.g., $\text{BF}_3$)

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Metallic bonding where valence electrons are delocalized across a lattice of metal cations
